publicListingSignals: no website field found in OSM pass
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Public listing has phone/address but no owned website field, so searchers do not get a controlled first impression, repair context, or reusable landing page before calling competitors.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Minimum-useful one-page call path / starter website with Council Bluffs location context and mobile tap-to-call.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific, easy-to-understand leak; local auto repair calls are high intent; no email means phone/manual route only, but the value proposition is simple.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Cream/orange Council Bluffs repair-counter and odometer motif, with Fifth Avenue location cue and no dark hero/right-form/stat-strip pattern.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA found the concept polished and usable but initially too agency/audit-like; hero and section copy were revised to be more customer-facing while preserving sample-site guardrails. Live production URL verification still depends on Vercel deploy.

draftCopy.subject: quick Council Bluffs repair page idea
draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — is this Harter's Service & Repair? I’m local-ish and noticed the public listing I found had the phone/address but I didn’t see a website link. I put together a small no-obligation sample page showing how the shop could have a simple mobile call path for Council Bluffs drivers. Worth polishing this up for you? If not, no worries — say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: no website field found in OSM pass
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Main Street listing appears to rely on directory discovery without an owned website field, limiting trust-building and call-routing control.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Starter one-page shop profile with phone-first mobile CTA and verified service/category copy.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: No-site signal is concrete and easy to explain; small-market businesses can benefit from a very simple owned page.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; would need manual confirmation of actual services and contact route before a useful page beats generic auto-shop copy.

draftCopy.subject: quick Main Street website idea
draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — is this J & J Motor Company? I found the public listing with the Main Street address and phone, but I didn’t see a website link. I’m putting together simple phone-first pages for local shops so people searching on mobile can call without bouncing through directories. Worth sketching one out for you? Reply/say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: page title: Auto Repair & Muffler Shop in Cedar Rapids, IA
first-page fetch did not expose tel links or obvious phone text
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: The site is live, but the automated first-page check did not expose a form, tel link, or obvious phone in HTML; if mobile visitors or crawlers miss the call path, high-intent repair/muffler searches can leak.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful conversion audit: verify mobile header/call tracking, tap-to-call markup, and a service-specific muffler/brake landing path.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: This is a non-accusatory helpful-alert lead, not a redesign pitch; the issue can be checked/fixed quickly if real.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created because the official site may already have visual assets/JS-rendered contact UI; outreach should lead with a verification question only.

draftCopy.subject: possible tap-to-call issue on Denny’s site
draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — I was doing a quick mobile/contact-path check for Denny’s Automotive & Muffler Center and noticed the first-page HTML I fetched didn’t show a form or obvious tap-to-call link, even though the site is live. It may just be a script/rendering thing, but it seemed worth flagging because auto-repair calls are so mobile-heavy. Want me to send the quick note/screenshot? Say NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Appointment-only detailing business has a basic GoDaddy page and public Gmail route, but the fetched first page did not show an embedded request/booking form for high-intent detail inquiries.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Detailing appointment landing page with packages/vehicle condition intake, photo upload prompt, and ad-ready conversion tracking.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Clear paid-ad/booking value without attacking the site; public Gmail gives a direct draft route after approval.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; a high-quality concept should first confirm actual detailing packages/photos so it does not look like a generic car-detail template.

draftCopy.subject: quick appointment-page idea for Five Seasons Detail
draftCopy.emailBody: Hi Five Seasons Detail — I was looking at local Cedar Rapids detailing pages and noticed your public listing points to the GoDaddy site + Gmail, with appointment-only hours. One idea that may help: a focused appointment page where people can pick the detail type, add vehicle/condition notes, and request a slot from a phone before you run any ads. I have not sent this anywhere — just a small SYBEORG concept/audit note. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: title: HVAC Experts in Northwest Iowa - D & L Plumbing & Heating
mailto not found in fetched HTML
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: The site is real and established, so the opportunity is not a homepage redesign; the gap is campaign readiness: no obvious first-page form in the fetch and room for seasonal HVAC/plumbing landing pages with tracking.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: One seasonal HVAC/plumbing campaign page with call tracking and a quote/request path, not a generic redesign.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: High-value trade vertical, public phone route, and a respectful angle that complements rather than criticizes their existing site.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created because a homepage concept would likely be weaker than a focused seasonal landing-page/audit proposal.

draftCopy.subject: seasonal HVAC page idea for D&L
draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — I was reviewing Northwest Iowa HVAC/plumbing sites and D&L’s site is definitely live, so this is not a redesign pitch. The opportunity I noticed is more specific: a seasonal campaign page for AC/furnace or plumbing requests with tap-to-call, a short request path, and tracking if you ever run ads. Worth sketching that up for you? Say NO and I will not follow up.

mockupsSkippedReason: Only one mockup was created because the other selected leads needed either manual service/contact verification or a more specific campaign/audit angle to avoid generic mockups.

outboundActions.notes: Drafts only; all outreach requires Stark approval.
